question:
Consider the following statements regarding the semiconductor manufacturing projects approved under ISM 1.0 in May 2026:

1. The Union Cabinet approved two semiconductor projects with an investment of around Rs 3,936 crore.
2. India's first commercial GaN Technology-based display facility will be set up by Suchi Semicon Private Limited.
3. The projects mark the final approvals under the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 1.0.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statement 2 is incorrect. The GaN Technology-based display facility is by Crystal Matrix Limited (CML), while the semiconductor packaging unit is by Suchi Semicon Private Limited (SSPL). Statements 1 and 3 are correct as per Page 6 of the PDF.

question: The Union Cabinet chaired by PM Narendra Modi approved the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) 5.0 to help MSMEs mitigate liquidity stress. What is the total financial outlay for this scheme?
rationale: The Union Cabinet approved Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) 5.0 with an outlay of Rs 18,100 crore. (Page 6)

question:
Fill in the blanks regarding the Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for Sugarcane for the 2026-27 Season:

The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs approved the proposal to increase the FRP to Rs ______ per quintal at a basic recovery rate of ______ %.
rationale: The FRP for sugarcane was increased to Rs 365 per quintal for the 2026-27 season, at a basic recovery rate of 10.25%. (Pages 6-7)

question: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the 'Scheme for Promotion of Surface Coal/Lignite Gasification Projects'?

options.A: It was approved with a total budget outlay of Rs 37,500 crore.
options.B: It aims to reduce India’s reliance on imports of Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG), urea, and methanol.
options.C: The scheme will offer a financial incentive of up to 40% of the cost of plant and machinery.
options.D: It is structured on the National Coal Gasification Mission (NCGM) launched in 2021.
options.E: The GoI has extended coal linkage tenure with a maximum time period of 30 years.

rationale: Option C is incorrect because the scheme will offer financial incentives up to 20% (not 40%) of the cost of plant and machinery. (Page 7)

question:
Match the following Kharif Crops with their respective absolute increase in Minimum Support Price (MSP) for the marketing season 2026-27:

Column I (Crop):
1. Sunflower seeds
2. Cotton
3. Sesamum

Column II (Increase in Rs per quintal):
A. Rs 500
B. Rs 557
C. Rs 622

Select the correct matching code:
rationale: The highest absolute increase in MSP was recommended for: Sunflower seeds at Rs 622/quintal, Cotton at Rs 557/quintal, and Sesamum at Rs 500/quintal. (Page 7)

question:
The Union Cabinet recently approved the continuation of the 'SARTHAK-PDS' scheme. Match the AI platforms deployed under the scheme with their respective functions:

Column I (AI Platform):
1. NIRMAL
2. ASHA
3. SAKSHAM

Column II (Function):
A. GPS tracking and logistics
B. Multilingual grievance redressal
C. Registry for detecting duplicate beneficiaries

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Under SARTHAK-PDS: NIRMAL is for detecting duplicate/ghost beneficiaries (registry), ASHA is for multilingual grievance redressal, and SAKSHAM is for logistics/GPS tracking. (Page 8)

question: In May 2026, the Bihar government approved a loan from the World Bank to bolster selected urban centres under the 'Bihar Urban Transformation Programme'. What is the value of this loan?
rationale: The Bihar cabinet approved a proposal of USD 500 million (or Rs 4,750 crore) loan from the World Bank for the 'Bihar Urban Transformation Programme'. (Page 8)

question: Which state government launched the Rs 200 Crore 'Adarsh Shahar Samriddhi Yojana' to develop modern civic infrastructure in emerging towns and urban centres?
rationale: The Government of Chhattisgarh, under CM Vishnu Deo Sai, launched the 'Adarsh Shahar Samriddhi Yojana' with an allocation of Rs 200 crore. (Page 8)

question: In April 2026, which city police introduced India’s first AI-powered multilingual 'Namma 112' Emergency Response Support System?
rationale: Bengaluru City Police launched a multilingual AI integration in the 'Namma 112' Emergency Response Support System, a first-of-its-kind initiative in India. (Page 8)

question: The Maharashtra Cabinet approved the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Policy 2026 with an aim to attract an investment of Rs __________ and generate 1.5 lakh employment opportunities by 2031.
rationale: The Maharashtra Cabinet approved the AI Policy 2026 aimed at attracting Rs 10,000 crore investment and generating 1.5 lakh employment opportunities by 2031. (Page 9)

question: Which Northeastern state launched the 'Khuarel' mobile application to boost real-time disaster response and coordination across the state?
rationale: The Mizoram government launched the 'Khuarel' mobile app, a disaster incident & reporting management system. (Page 9)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the '100 Days Ayushman Student Coverage Mission':

1. It was launched by the Government of Nagaland.
2. It aims to expand health insurance coverage among students.
3. It is an extension of the state's own Chief Minister’s Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS) and AB-PMJAY.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. The Government of Nagaland launched the '100 Days Ayushman Student Coverage Mission' to expand youth health coverage as an extension of AB-PMJAY and CMHIS. (Page 9)

question: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari launched India’s first Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) barrier-less tolling system. This system is located on the Surat–Bharuch section of which National Highway?
rationale: India's first barrier-less tolling system was launched at the Chorayasi Toll Plaza on the Surat–Bharuch section of National Highways (NH)-48 in Gujarat. (Page 9)

question:
Assertion (A): Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia launched the 'Cell Broadcast System (CBS)' to warn citizens about emergency and natural disasters.

Reason (R): The system was developed exclusively by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) without any external partnerships.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true, but R is false.
options.D: A is false, but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Assertion (A) is true. Reason (R) is false because the newly launched system has been developed by the Centre for Development of Telematics (C-DOT) in partnership with NDMA and MHA. (Pages 9-10)

question: Under the framework of the Trade & Technology Council (TTC), India and the European Union launched a EUR 15.2 million initiative to boost recycling of which of the following?
rationale: India and the EU announced the launch of a third coordinated call for proposals focused on the Recycling of Electric Vehicle (EV) Batteries with a funding pool of 15.2 million Euros. (Page 10)

question: The Ministry of Earth Sciences established India’s first comprehensive Urban Testbed and Aerosol Observatory in which city?
rationale: The Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune established India’s first comprehensive Urban Testbed and Aerosol Observatory at the SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) in Ramapuram, Chennai, TN. (Page 10)

question: Union Ministers Jagat Prakash Nadda and Shivraj Singh Chouhan jointly launched the 'SEHAT Mission'. What does SEHAT stand for?

options.A: Science Excellence for Health through Agricultural Transformation
options.B: Scientific Empowerment for Health and Agricultural Technology
options.C: Sustainable Ecosystems for Health and Agrarian Training
options.D: Science and Environment for Healthy Agricultural Transformation
options.E: Systematic Excellence in Health and Agricultural Transformation

rationale: The 'SEHAT Mission' stands for 'Science Excellence for Health through Agricultural Transformation'. It links agriculture, nutrition, and public health. (Page 10)

question: The Department of Financial Services (DFS) launched the 'Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool (BMIP)' to secure shipping risks of Indian vessels. What is the total size of the BMIP?
rationale: DFS launched the 'Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool (BMIP)' of USD 1.5 Billion, with a sovereign guarantee of USD 1.4 Bn. (Page 11)

question: India’s first integrated Carbon Capture, Utilisation and Storage (CCUS) field laboratory facility was inaugurated by Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan at which institution?
rationale: Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan inaugurated India’s first integrated CCUS field laboratory facility at Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay (IIT-B). (Page 11)

question: The Government of India and the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) launched a new Country Strategic Opportunities Programme (COSOP) for which of the following periods?
rationale: GoI and IFAD officially launched their new eight-year Country Strategic Opportunities Programme (COSOP) for the period 2026–2033. (Page 11)

question: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the IP Catalyst initiative to transform patents into products. Which of the following is the official digital platform accompanying this initiative?
rationale: MeitY launched the Intellectual Property (IP) Catalyst initiative and its accompanying digital platform (https://cipie.in). (Page 11)

question: Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy unveiled the Unified Bharat e-Charge (UBC) platform. Under the PM E-DRIVE Scheme, how many EV charging stations were approved for installation in Karnataka?
rationale: Union Minister H. D. Kumaraswamy approved the installation of 1,243 EV charging stations in Karnataka with a financial outlay of Rs 123.26 crore. (Page 12)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'Project Saksham':

1. It was launched by the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) in collaboration with the Vertis Foundation.
2. It aims to empower rural women through skill development and employment opportunities.
3. It operates through 12 training centres across India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. Project Saksham was launched by NHAI and Vertis Foundation to empower rural women through 12 training centres. (Page 12)

question:
Union Minister Jyotiraditya M. Scindia launched the 'Coffee of Nagaland' mission. Match the pilot clusters identified in Nagaland with the type of coffee they will cultivate:

Column I (Village):
1. Tuophema village
2. Ghotovi village

Column II (Coffee Type):
A. Robusta coffee
B. Arabica coffee

Select the correct matching code:
rationale: Under the 'Coffee of Nagaland' mission, Tuophema village in Kohima district is identified for Arabica coffee, and Ghotovi village in Niuland district is identified for Robusta coffee. (Page 12)

question: The National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS) launched an initiative with the mission 'To Reach the Unreached'. What is the primary objective of this initiative?

options.A: To provide free digital tablets to rural students
options.B: To reconnect out-of-school children with the formal education system
options.C: To train teachers in AI and machine learning
options.D: To provide mid-day meals to open school students
options.E: To build smart classrooms in remote areas

rationale: The mission of the initiative is 'To Reach the Unreached', and NIOS aims to reconnect out-of-school children (especially 14-18 age group) with the formal education system under NEP 2020. (Page 12)

question: What is the name of the Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based helpbot launched by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to protect citizens from rising cyber fraud and digital arrest scams?
rationale: CBI launched an AI-based helpbot named 'ABHAY' (AI-Based Helpbot for Authentication of Your Notice) to authenticate CBI notices and combat digital arrest scams. (Page 13)

question: Which state was the focus of the Rs 167 crore cluster-based mission designed specifically for Kiwi cultivation and value chain development launched by Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia?
rationale: The mission is titled 'Arunachal Kiwi: The USP of Arunachal Pradesh', designed specifically for Arunachal Pradesh with an outlay of Rs 167 crore. (Page 13)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Phase-II of the UMMID Programme:

1. It focuses on the management and treatment of Rare Genetic Disorders (RGDs).
2. It was launched under the Department of Biotechnology (DBT).
3. Phase-II includes the establishment of 25 additional NIDAN Kendras across 13 states and one UT.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. The UMMID Phase-II for Rare Genetic Disorders was launched under DBT and expands by 25 NIDAN Kendras. (Page 13)

question: The United Kingdom's development finance institution (BII) and Denmark's Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) launched a USD 300 million Renewable Energy platform in India named:
rationale: BII and CIP launched 'North Star', a USD 300 million Renewable Energy (RE) platform aimed at supporting India’s clean energy transition. (Page 13)

question: The Bharath Digital Infrastructure Association (BDIA) launched 'Bharat Digital Samvad', promoting a core principle highlighting India’s sovereign right to control its data. What is this principle called?
rationale: BDIA launched Bharat Digital Samvad promoting the principle of 'Data Swaraj', highlighting India's sovereign right to control its data. (Page 14)

question: The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) launched an Innovator–Industry Platform under the 'Medical Innovations Patent Mitra' event. How many public health technologies were transferred to industry partners during this event?
rationale: ICMR launched a first-of-its-kind Innovator-Industry Platform that transfers 41 Public Health Technologies to Industry. (Page 14)

question: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia launched the Rs 236 crore 'Mission Queen Pineapple' in which state?
rationale: Union Minister Jyotiraditya M. Scindia launched 'Tripura Mission Queen Pineapple', a Rs.236 crore initiative in Tripura. (Page 14)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Logistics Port Performance Index (LPPI) launched by Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal:

1. It was launched during the 37th Foundation Day celebrations of the Jawaharlal Nehru Port Authority (JNPA).
2. It evaluates port performance across three main cargo segments: dry bulk, liquid bulk, and container cargo.
3. It uses exactly 10 key operational metrics to assess the ports.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statement 3 is incorrect because the LPPI uses 6 key operational metrics, not 10. Statements 1 and 2 are correct. (Page 14)

question: DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V. Kamat inaugurated a CBRN Field Training and Demonstration Centre to strengthen nuclear emergency preparedness. In which city is this facility located?
rationale: The CBRN Field Training and Demonstration Centre was inaugurated at Burari plains in New Delhi, Delhi. (Page 15)

question: The 'Bhimmandali Eco Heritage Site', for which Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan laid the foundation stone, is located in which district of Odisha?
rationale: The Bhimmandali Eco Heritage Site is located under the Naktideul Block of Sambalpur district in Odisha. (Page 15)

question: India’s 1st SME-led semiconductor ATMP/OSAT facility, established by Sahasra Semiconductors Pvt. Ltd., was inaugurated in which state?
rationale: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw virtually inaugurated India’s 1st SME-led semiconductor ATMP/OSAT facility at Bhiwadi, Rajasthan. (Page 15)

question: Union Minister Rajnath Singh flagged off India’s first 300-km Universal Rocket Launching System named 'Suryastra' during the inauguration of a defence complex. The complex is located in:
rationale: Union Minister Rajnath Singh inaugurated the NIBE Group’s private defence manufacturing complex in Shirdi, Maharashtra, and flagged off 'Suryastra'. (Page 16)

question: Indian Railways inaugurated the 'CHIRAG' Centre of Excellence. What is the primary focus of this centre?
rationale: CHIRAG (Centre for HR Information Systems, Research and Analytics in Governance) is dedicated to Human Resource Management training, research, and digital transformation in Indian Railways. (Page 16)

question: Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh inaugurated the PECVD System and RETINA incubation centre at the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science (IACS) campus. In which city is IACS located?
rationale: The initiatives were inaugurated at the IACS campus in Kolkata, West Bengal, on the occasion of its 150 years celebrations. (Page 16)

question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the newly notified Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026?

options.A: Minor children cannot hold both Indian and foreign passports simultaneously.
options.B: The rules introduce physical e-OCI cards while abolishing digital records.
options.C: The entire OCI application process will be conducted online.
options.D: OCI card surrender must be filed electronically.
options.E: It ensures compliance with India's single citizenship policy.

rationale: Option B is incorrect because the rules introduce e-OCI *along with* digital records, allowing issuance of physical or electronic OCI cards and maintaining a central database. (Page 16)

question: The Ministry of Mines approved 58 companies under the Incentive Scheme for Promotion of Critical Mineral Recycling (IS-PCMR). What is the total outlay of this scheme?
rationale: The IS-PCMR scheme is a key initiative under the National Critical Mineral Mission (NCMM) with a total outlay of Rs 1,500 crore. (Page 17)

question: The Open-Field Observatory of the Coastal Atmospheric Research Testbed (C-ART) was inaugurated at which university?
rationale: The C-ART was inaugurated at Andhra University (AU), Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. (Page 17)

question: Which state recently became the first state in India to have a fully paperless judiciary?
rationale: Sikkim became the first state in India to have a fully paperless judiciary, announced during its 50 years of statehood. (Page 17)

question: Under the Humanitarian Diplomacy And Relief (HADR) framework, the Government of India deployed its 'Aarogya Maitri' (BHISHM Cube) healthcare system to which country?
rationale: India deployed its Aarogya Maitri healthcare system in Jamaica, strengthening ties with Caribbean nations. (Page 17)

question:
Arrange the following financial years in the correct chronological order corresponding to the Ministry of Power's phased local content targets (30%, 40%, 50%, 60%) for HVDC substations:

1. FY30
2. FY28
3. FY35
4. FY32

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: The phased local content targets are 30% by FY28, 40% by FY30, 50% by FY32, and 60% by FY35. Therefore, the order is FY28 (2), FY30 (1), FY32 (4), FY35 (3). (Page 17)

question:
Match the following Indian Standards (IS) published by BIS under the NLEAP Initiative with their respective assistive technologies:

Column I:
1. IS 18558 Part 1: 2025
2. IS 19190: 2025
3. IS 19631: 2026

Column II:
A. Braille on Signage
B. Portable Ramps
C. Elbow Crutches

Select the correct matching code:
rationale: IS 18558 Part 1: 2025 is for Elbow Crutches; IS 19190: 2025 is for Braille on Signage; IS 19631: 2026 is for Portable Ramps. (Pages 17-18)

question: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare introduced the '4Ts' awareness framework for the early detection of Type 1 Diabetes in children. Which of the following is NOT one of the '4Ts'?
rationale: The 4Ts framework includes Toilet, Thirsty, Tired, and Thinner. 'Trembling' is not one of them. (Page 18)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the newly notified South Coast Railway (SCoR) Zone:

1. It will become India's 18th railway zone.
2. Its headquarters will be located at Visakhapatnam.
3. It is set to be operational from 1 June 2026.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct. The SCoR Zone, headquartered at Visakhapatnam, will be India's 18th railway zone, operational from 1 June 2026. (Page 18)

question: Fill in the blank: The DPIIT has issued a revised framework for expedited clearance of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) proposals in 40 sub-sectors from countries sharing a land border with India. These proposals will now be processed within ______ days.
rationale: According to the updated SOP, FDI proposals from countries sharing a land border in these sub-sectors will be processed within 60 days. (Page 18)

question: The MoHFW launched the Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) 2.0 Guidelines. What age group of children does this health screening and early intervention programme cover?
rationale: The RBSK 2.0 marks a major expansion of India’s child health screening and early intervention programme for children from birth to 18 years. (Page 19)

question: India's first green methanol production plant is being set up at Deendayal Port Authority (DPA) in Gujarat. Which invasive shrub will be converted into green marine fuel at this plant?
rationale: The plant will convert the invasive Prosopis juliflora shrub, locally known as Gando Baval, into green marine fuel. (Page 19)

question: The National Statistics Office (NSO) notified uniform guidelines for compiling Gross State Value Added (GSVA). What is the newly updated base year for calculating State GDP?
rationale: The NSO notified uniform guidelines with 2022-23 as the new base year for compiling GSVA, replacing the previous 2011-12 base year. (Page 19)

question: CSIR-CRRI in collaboration with BPCL developed India’s first technical textile geocell named 'Phoenix'. What is 'Phoenix' entirely made from?
rationale: Phoenix Geocell is India’s first technical textile geocell made entirely from end-of-life mixed plastic waste. (Page 19)

question: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) renewed a 5-year MoU to strengthen elite youth football development with the government of which state?
rationale: AIFF and the Government of Odisha signed a renewed five-year MoU for youth football development. (Page 19)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'One Case One Data' initiative announced by CJI Surya Kant:

1. It will assign a single unique digital identity (ID) to each case.
2. It integrates multi-level information from high courts, district, and taluka courts into a unified system.
3. An AI chatbot named 'Su Sahay' was launched alongside it for judicial assistance.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: All statements are correct regarding the 'One Case One Data' initiative and the AI chatbot 'Su Sahay'. (Page 19-20)

question: The implementation of the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) [VB-G RAM G] Act, 2025 provides a statutory guarantee of how many days of work in every financial year?
rationale: The new act provides a statutory guarantee of 125 days of work in every financial year, up from the provision of 100 days in MGNREGA. (Page 20)

question: India assumed the chairmanship of the Common Criteria Development Board (CCDB) for a period of two years. Which organization represents India as the official body for IT security certification in the CCRA?
rationale: India is represented by MeitY and the Standardisation Testing and Quality Certification (STQC) Directorate as the official body for IT security certification. (Page 20)

question: Which state became the first in India to complete all 51 priority areas identified under Deregulation Phase-I and Phase-II aimed at improving ease of doing business?
rationale: Tripura became the first state in India to complete all 51 priority areas under Deregulation Phase–I and II. (Page 21)

question: India’s First AI-Powered Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicle (UCAV) 'Kaal Bhairava' will be manufactured at its first international production facility in which country?
rationale: Flying Wedge Defence & Aerospace (FWDA) announced that 'Kaal Bhairava' will be manufactured at its first international production facility in Portugal. (Page 21)

question: According to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry (MoC&I), which region emerged as India’s third-largest seafood export market during the Financial Year 2025-26?
rationale: The European Union (EU) emerged as India’s third-largest seafood export market in FY26, after the USA and China. (Page 21)

question: India’s 1st Solar-Powered 'Algae Tree', designed to tackle air pollution by utilizing microalgae to absorb carbon dioxide, was introduced in which city?
rationale: India's first 'Algae Tree' was inaugurated at Swami Vivekananda Park in Bhopal, MP. (Page 21)

question: Who has been appointed as the Chairperson of the parliamentary Committee on Empowerment of Women for the year 2026–27 constituted by Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla?
rationale: Dr. Daggubati Purandeswari, MP and Senior BJP leader, has been appointed as the Chairperson of the committee. (Page 21)

question: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) notified fuel specifications for blending petrol with ethanol up to what maximum percentage under the newly established norms (IS 19850:2026)?
rationale: BIS notified specifications for E22, E25, E27, and E30 fuel blends, approving up to 30% of ethanol blending in petrol. (Page 22)

question:
Assertion (A): The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) introduced Retirement Income Schemes (RIS) under NPS to provide flexible payout options post-retirement.

Reason (R): Under the new framework, NPS subscribers are strictly required to use at least 80% of their corpus to purchase an annuity.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true, but R is false.
options.D: A is false, but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Assertion (A) is true. Reason (R) is false because currently, subscribers withdraw up to 60% tax-free and must use at least 40% (not 80%) to purchase an annuity. The new RIS provides more flexibility rather than forcing an 80% annuity rule. (Page 26)

question: What is the total financial outlay for the BHAVYA (Bharat Audyogik Vikas Yojna) Scheme according to the guidelines released by DPIIT?
rationale: The total financial outlay for the BHAVYA scheme is Rs 33,660 crore, which includes an administrative fund of Rs 60 Cr. (Page 26)

question:
Arrange the following destinations of PM Narendra Modi's 5-nation tour in May 2026 in the correct chronological sequence of his visits:

1. Italy
2. United Arab Emirates (UAE)
3. Norway
4. Netherlands
5. Sweden

Select the correct sequence:
rationale: The PM's 5-nation tour sequence was: UAE (May 15), Netherlands (May 16-17), Sweden (May 17-18), Norway (May 18-19), and Italy (May 19-20). The sequence is 2, 4, 5, 3, 1. (Pages 29-31)

question: As per the final guidelines issued by RBI for NBFCs not availing public funds and not having customer interface, entities with an asset size of less than what amount will be classified as 'Unregistered Type I NBFCs'?
rationale: Entities that do not avail of public funds, have no customer interface, and have an asset size of less than Rs 1,000 crore are classified as 'Unregistered Type I NBFCs'. (Page 32)

question: The RBI approved a record surplus transfer (dividend) to the Government of India for the Financial Year 2025-26. What is the amount of this dividend?
rationale: The RBI approved a record surplus transfer of Rs 2,86,588.46 crore (approx Rs 2.87 lakh crore) to GoI for FY26. (Page 33)

question: Which of the following frameworks was operationalised by SEBI to independently validate performance claims made by regulated intermediaries and unregistered finfluencers?
rationale: SEBI operationalised the 'Past Risk and Return Verification Agency (PaRRVA) framework' to enhance transparency in performance claims. (Page 34)

question:
Match the following banks/financial institutions with the entities they partnered with in May 2026:

Column I:
1. Jio Payments Bank
2. Airtel Payments Bank
3. West Bengal Gramin Bank

Column II:
A. Delhi Metro Rail Corporation
B. Ezeepay
C. Canara HSBC Life Insurance

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Jio Payments Bank partnered with Ezeepay. Airtel Payments Bank partnered with Delhi Metro Rail Corporation. West Bengal Gramin Bank partnered with Canara HSBC Life Insurance. (Pages 34-35)
